claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children you have
- Their names (or you can use Child 1, Child 2, etc. if you prefer)
- Their ages

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I have one child - Alex, who's 18 years old and lives with me at home. I wanted to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is 18 years old, this is an important factor. Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16 and over depends on whether they're in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** For example:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, T-levels, Scottish Highers)
- Approved training programs
- Similar approved courses

Please note that university or higher education courses don't qualify.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school and aren't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that information.

Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with the outcome:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit until a child turns 16, or up until they turn 20 if they remain in approved education or training (such as A-levels, T-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved training programs - but not university).

Since Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in approved education or training, they don't meet the eligibility requirements.

**However, please note:** If Alex were to start approved education or training in the future (before turning 20), you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit again. This would need to be full-time non-advanced education or an approved training program.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and not in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit until a child turns 16, or up until they turn 20 if they remain in approved education or training (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved training programs, but not university). Since Alex is 18 and not in approved education or training, they do not meet the age and education requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and not currently in approved education or training. As Alex exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit (16, or 20 if in approved education/training) and is not in qualifying education, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
